text.title: Playwright Core API
text.summary: Auto-wait model, locator hierarchy, form handling, network interception, and browser context patterns.

rule.statement: Always prefer locators in this order: getByRole > getByLabel > getByText > getByTestId > CSS selectors. Role-based locators survive UI refactors; CSS selectors break on next sprint.
rule.rationale: getByRole uses the accessibility tree — the same API screen readers use. Accessibility-tree selectors are resilient to class renames, DOM restructuring, and style changes. CSS selectors break when any of these change.

antipattern.description: Using CSS selectors from codegen output (.btn-primary, #form-submit) without converting to role locators.
antipattern.reason: Codegen generates CSS as a lowest-common-denominator fallback. These selectors die when styling changes, causing flaky tests that have nothing to do with functionality.

section.title: Auto-Wait
section.p: Playwright automatically waits for elements to be actionable (visible, enabled, stable) before click/fill/check. No explicit waits needed for most operations.

rule.statement: Never use page.waitForTimeout(). Use expect(locator).toBeVisible({timeout}) or page.waitForLoadState() instead.
rule.rationale: waitForTimeout is a fixed sleep that causes flaky tests — too short on slow machines, too long on fast ones. Locator auto-wait adapts to actual readiness.

section.title: Network Interception
section.p: Block resources: await page.route('**/*.{png,jpg}', route => route.abort())
Mock responses: await page.route('**/api/users', route => route.fulfill({status:200, body:...}))
Wait for specific response: const resp = page.waitForResponse('**/api/data'); await page.click('#load'); const data = await (await resp).json();

section.title: Auth Reuse via storageState

rule.statement: Authenticate once via global setup and save to storageState. Reuse across all tests. Never login per-test — it adds 5x slowdown and rate-limit risk.
rule.rationale: Login per-test is the most common source of Playwright test suite slowdown. storageState serializes cookies and localStorage; subsequent tests start already authenticated.

section.p: Add auth.json to .gitignore — it contains session tokens that must not be committed to git.
